abandon.en: leave a person or place permanently, especially when you should stay

ex.en: The family had to abandon their home during the flood.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 홍수 때문에 집을 버리고 떠나야 했다.

abandon.en: stop doing or continuing something you have started

ex.en: They abandoned the project due to a lack of funding.
ex.ko: 그들은 자금 부족으로 그 프로젝트를 포기했다.

able.en: having the skill or opportunity to do something

ex.en: She is able to speak three languages.
ex.ko: 그녀는 세 개 언어를 할 수 있다.

abnormal.en: different from what is usual or expected, especially in a worrying way

ex.en: The doctor noticed abnormal growth in the cells.
ex.ko: 의사는 세포에서 비정상적인 성장을 발견했다.

aboard.en: on or into a ship, train, plane, or other vehicle

ex.en: All passengers must be aboard by 8 a.m.
ex.ko: 모든 승객은 오전 8시까지 탑승해야 한다.

aborigine.en: one of the people who were the original inhabitants of a country, especially Australia

ex.en: The museum has an exhibition about Aborigine art and culture.
ex.ko: 그 박물관에는 호주 원주민 예술과 문화에 관한 전시가 있다.

absence.en: the fact of something not existing or not being present

ex.en: In the absence of clear evidence, the police could not make an arrest.
ex.ko: 명확한 증거가 없어서 경찰은 체포할 수 없었다.

absence.en: a time when someone is not at school, work, or a place they should be

ex.en: Her absence from class worried the teacher.
ex.ko: 그녀의 결석은 선생님을 걱정시켰다.

absent.en: not present at a place where you are usually expected to be

ex.en: Three students were absent from school today.
ex.ko: 오늘 학생 세 명이 결석했다.

absolute.en: complete and total, without any exceptions or limits

ex.en: The judge has absolute power in the courtroom.
ex.ko: 판사는 법정에서 절대적인 권한을 가진다.

ex.en: I absolutely love this song.
ex.ko: 나는 이 노래가 완전히 좋다.

ex.en: "Do you want to come?" "Absolutely!"
ex.ko: "올래?" "물론이지!"

absorb.en: take in a liquid, gas, or other substance gradually

ex.en: The towel absorbed all the water quickly.
ex.ko: 그 수건은 물을 빠르게 다 흡수했다.

absorb.en: take up all of someone's attention or time completely

ex.en: She was completely absorbed in her book.
ex.ko: 그녀는 책에 완전히 몰두해 있었다.

abstract.en: existing as an idea rather than a physical or concrete thing

ex.en: Freedom is an abstract concept that is hard to define.
ex.ko: 자유는 정의하기 어려운 추상적인 개념이다.

abstract.en: a short summary of an academic paper or article

ex.en: Please read the abstract before the full report.
ex.ko: 전체 보고서를 읽기 전에 초록을 먼저 읽어 주세요.

abundance.en: a very large quantity of something

ex.en: The region has an abundance of natural resources.
ex.ko: 그 지역은 천연자원이 풍부하다.

ex.en: Fish are abundant in this river.
ex.ko: 이 강에는 물고기가 풍부하다.

academic.en: relating to education, schools, or universities

ex.en: She has a strong academic record.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학업 성적이 우수하다.

academy.en: a school, especially one for special training

ex.en: He graduated from a military academy.
ex.ko: 그는 육군사관학교를 졸업했다.

academy.en: an organization that works to develop and support a particular subject, such as science or art

ex.en: She was elected to the National Academy of Sciences.
ex.ko: 그녀는 국립과학아카데미 회원으로 선출되었다.

accent.en: the way a person pronounces words, showing which country or region they are from

ex.en: She speaks English with a French accent.
ex.ko: 그녀는 프랑스식 억양으로 영어를 한다.

accent.en: a detail that adds a distinctive style or emphasis to something

ex.en: The white walls had a red accent on one side.
ex.ko: 흰 벽에는 한쪽에 빨간색 포인트가 있었다.

acceptance.en: the act of agreeing to accept or receive something

ex.en: His acceptance of the job offer surprised everyone.
ex.ko: 그가 그 일자리 제안을 수락한 것은 모두를 놀라게 했다.

access.en: the right or opportunity to use or enter something

ex.en: Students have access to the library after school.
ex.ko: 학생들은 방과 후에 도서관을 이용할 수 있다.

access.en: reach, enter, or make use of something

ex.en: You can access your account online.
ex.ko: 온라인으로 계정에 접근할 수 있다.

accessible.en: able to be easily reached, entered, or obtained

ex.en: The new building is accessible to people in wheelchairs.
ex.ko: 그 새 건물은 휠체어를 탄 사람들도 이용하기 쉽다.

accessory.en: an extra item added to something to make it more useful, complete, or attractive

ex.en: She wore a lot of accessories, like earrings and bracelets.
ex.ko: 그녀는 귀걸이, 팔찌 같은 액세서리를 많이 착용했다.

accidental.en: happening by chance, not planned or intended

ex.en: The fire was accidental, not caused on purpose.
ex.ko: 그 화재는 고의가 아니라 우연히 일어난 것이었다.

ex.en: I accidentally deleted the file.
ex.ko: 나는 실수로 그 파일을 삭제했다.

ex.en: He accompanied his sister to the hospital.
ex.ko: 그는 여동생과 함께 병원에 갔다.

accompany.en: happen or exist at the same time as something else

ex.en: The storm was accompanied by strong winds.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍우는 강한 바람을 동반했다.

accomplish.en: succeed in doing or finishing something

ex.en: She accomplished all her goals this year.
ex.ko: 그녀는 올해 모든 목표를 성취했다.

according to.en: as stated by someone or something

ex.en: According to the report, sales increased last month.
ex.ko: 보고서에 따르면 지난달 매출이 증가했다.

accountant.en: a person whose job is to keep or check financial accounts

ex.en: She works as an accountant for a law firm.
ex.ko: 그녀는 로펌에서 회계사로 일한다.

accuracy.en: the quality of being correct or exact

ex.en: The report was praised for its accuracy.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 정확성 면에서 좋은 평가를 받았다.

accurate.en: correct and exact, without any mistakes

ex.en: The weather forecast was surprisingly accurate.
ex.ko: 일기예보가 놀라울 만큼 정확했다.

accurately.en: in a correct and exact way

ex.en: He described the accident accurately.
ex.ko: 그는 사고를 정확하게 설명했다.

accuse.en: say that someone has done something wrong or illegal

ex.en: He was accused of stealing money.
ex.ko: 그는 돈을 훔쳤다고 고발당했다.

accustom.en: make someone used to a new situation or experience

ex.en: It took time to accustom myself to the new schedule.
ex.ko: 새 일정에 익숙해지는 데 시간이 걸렸다.

ache.en: a continuous dull pain in a part of the body

ex.en: I have a dull ache in my back.
ex.ko: 나는 등에 은근한 통증이 있다.

ex.en: My legs ache after running.
ex.ko: 달리고 나면 다리가 아프다.

achievement.en: something that has been done successfully through hard work or skill

ex.en: Winning the award was a great achievement for her.
ex.ko: 그 상을 받은 것은 그녀에게 큰 업적이었다.

acknowledge.en: accept or admit that something is true

ex.en: He acknowledged his mistake in the meeting.
ex.ko: 그는 회의에서 자신의 실수를 인정했다.

acknowledge.en: confirm that you have received something, such as a letter or message

ex.en: Please acknowledge receipt of this email.
ex.ko: 이 이메일을 받았음을 확인해 주세요.

acknowledgment.en: the act of accepting that something is true, or an expression of thanks

ex.en: She received an acknowledgment for her contribution to the project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 프로젝트에 기여한 것에 대해 감사의 표시를 받았다.

acquaintance.en: a person you know but who is not a close friend

ex.en: He's just a business acquaintance, not a close friend.
ex.ko: 그는 그냥 사업상 아는 사람이지, 친한 친구는 아니다.

acquire.en: gain a skill, quality, or piece of knowledge over time

ex.en: He acquired basic cooking skills from his mother.
ex.ko: 그는 어머니에게서 기본적인 요리 기술을 습득했다.

acquire.en: obtain or buy something, such as property or a company

ex.en: The firm acquired a smaller competitor last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 더 작은 경쟁사를 인수했다.

active.en: doing a lot of things, or full of energy and action

ex.en: He stays active by going to the gym every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 헬스장에 가서 활동적으로 지낸다.

actress.en: a woman who acts in films, television, or theater

ex.en: She became a famous actress after that movie.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 영화 이후 유명한 여배우가 되었다.

ex.en: I saw an ad for a new phone on TV.
ex.ko: 나는 TV에서 새 휴대폰 광고를 봤다.

adapt.en: change your behavior to deal with a new situation

ex.en: It takes time to adapt to a new culture.
ex.ko: 새로운 문화에 적응하는 데는 시간이 걸린다.

adapt.en: change a book or story so it can be used as a film, play, or TV show

ex.en: The novel was adapted into a popular movie.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 인기 있는 영화로 각색되었다.

addressee.en: the person that a letter or message is sent to

ex.en: Make sure the addressee's name is spelled correctly.
ex.ko: 수신인 이름 철자가 맞는지 확인하세요.

administration.en: the process of managing or organizing a business, institution, or system

ex.en: She works in the administration department of the hospital.
ex.ko: 그녀는 병원 행정 부서에서 일한다.

administration.en: the government of a country, especially during a particular president's or leader's term

ex.en: The new administration announced tax reforms.
ex.ko: 새 행정부는 세제 개혁을 발표했다.

admiration.en: a feeling of respect and approval for someone or something

ex.en: I have great admiration for her courage.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀의 용기에 큰 감탄을 느낀다.

admission.en: the act of being allowed to enter a place, school, or organization

ex.en: Admission to the museum is free on Sundays.
ex.ko: 일요일에는 박물관 입장이 무료이다.

admission.en: a statement admitting that something is true, especially something bad

ex.en: His admission of guilt surprised the court.
ex.ko: 그의 죄를 인정하는 자백은 법정을 놀라게 했다.

adopt.en: legally take another person's child into your family as your own

ex.en: They decided to adopt a baby girl.
ex.ko: 그들은 여자 아기를 입양하기로 결정했다.

adopt.en: choose to start using a particular method, policy, or attitude

ex.en: The company adopted a new marketing strategy.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새로운 마케팅 전략을 채택했다.

adorable.en: very attractive and easy to love, especially because of being cute

ex.en: Their new puppy is absolutely adorable.
ex.ko: 그들의 새 강아지는 정말 사랑스럽다.

advance.en: move forward, or help something make progress

ex.en: Scientists have advanced our understanding of the disease.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 그 질병에 대한 이해를 진전시켰다.

advance.en: a development or improvement in something

ex.en: There have been many advances in medical technology.
ex.ko: 의료 기술에서 많은 발전이 있었다.

advance.en: an amount of money paid before it is earned or owed

ex.en: He asked his boss for a small advance on his salary.
ex.ko: 그는 상사에게 월급 선불을 조금 요청했다.

adverb.en: a word that describes or adds information to a verb, adjective, or another adverb

ex.en: 'Quickly' is an adverb that describes how she ran.
ex.ko: 'quickly'는 그녀가 어떻게 달렸는지 설명하는 부사이다.

adverbial.en: acting like or relating to an adverb

ex.en: 'In the morning' is an adverbial phrase of time.
ex.ko: 'in the morning'은 시간을 나타내는 부사구이다.

ex.en: I saw an advert for a sale on the radio.
ex.ko: 나는 라디오에서 세일 광고를 들었다.

advertise.en: tell people publicly about a product or service in order to sell it

ex.en: The company advertises its products on social media.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 소셜 미디어에서 제품을 광고한다.

adviser.en: a person whose job is to give expert advice

ex.en: She works as a financial adviser.
ex.ko: 그녀는 재무 고문으로 일한다.

aerobics.en: exercise, often done to music, that increases the amount of oxygen your body uses

ex.en: She does aerobics three times a week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 일주일에 세 번 에어로빅을 한다.

affect.en: have an influence on someone or something, often causing change

ex.en: The heavy rain affected the football match.
ex.ko: 폭우가 그 축구 경기에 영향을 미쳤다.

affection.en: a feeling of liking or caring for someone

ex.en: He has great affection for his grandmother.
ex.ko: 그는 할머니에게 큰 애정을 가지고 있다.

afford.en: have enough money, time, or ability to do something

ex.en: We can't afford a new car this year.
ex.ko: 우리는 올해 새 차를 살 여유가 없다.

afterward.en: at a later time than something already mentioned

ex.en: We had dinner and went to a movie afterward.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁을 먹고 그 후에 영화를 보러 갔다.

afterwards.en: at a later time than something already mentioned

ex.en: We had dinner and went to a movie afterwards.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁을 먹고 그 후에 영화를 보러 갔다.

agenda.en: a list of matters to be discussed at a meeting

ex.en: The budget is the first item on the agenda.
ex.ko: 예산이 안건의 첫 번째 항목이다.

aggressive.en: behaving in an angry or forceful way, ready to attack or argue

ex.en: The dog became aggressive when strangers approached.
ex.ko: 낯선 사람이 다가오자 그 개는 공격적으로 변했다.

agreement.en: an arrangement or decision that two or more people or groups have agreed on

ex.en: The two companies reached an agreement on the price.
ex.ko: 두 회사는 가격에 대해 합의에 도달했다.

ex.en: This is an agricultural region known for its rice fields.
ex.ko: 이곳은 논으로 유명한 농업 지역이다.

agriculture.en: the practice of growing crops and raising animals for food

ex.en: Agriculture is the main industry in this area.
ex.ko: 농업은 이 지역의 주요 산업이다.

aid.en: help, especially money, food, or equipment given to people in need

ex.en: The country received foreign aid after the earthquake.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 지진 이후 해외 원조를 받았다.

ex.en: Volunteers aided the victims of the flood.
ex.ko: 자원봉사자들은 홍수 피해자들을 도왔다.

aim.en: something that you plan to achieve

ex.en: Her aim is to become a doctor.
ex.ko: 그녀의 목표는 의사가 되는 것이다.

aim.en: point a weapon or object at a target

ex.en: He aimed the camera at the sunset.
ex.ko: 그는 카메라를 노을 쪽으로 겨냥했다.

aim.en: plan or intend to achieve something

ex.en: The company aims to double its sales this year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 올해 매출을 두 배로 늘리는 것을 목표로 한다.

air conditioning.en: a system that cools and dries the air inside a building or vehicle

ex.en: Please turn on the air conditioning; it's so hot.
ex.ko: 너무 더우니까 에어컨 좀 틀어 주세요.

air force.en: the part of a country's military that fights using aircraft

ex.en: His father served in the air force for twenty years.
ex.ko: 그의 아버지는 20년 동안 공군에서 복무했다.

airline.en: a company that provides flights for passengers

ex.en: Which airline are you flying with?
ex.ko: 어느 항공사 비행기를 타나요?

alcohol.cue: 술에 들어있는 성분, 또는 술 자체
alcohol.en: a colorless liquid found in drinks such as beer and wine that can make people drunk

ex.en: He doesn't drink alcohol for religious reasons.
ex.ko: 그는 종교적인 이유로 술을 마시지 않는다.

ex.en: They only serve alcoholic drinks after 6 p.m.
ex.ko: 그들은 오후 6시 이후에만 알코올이 든 음료를 제공한다.

alcoholic.en: a person who is addicted to drinking alcohol

ex.en: His father was an alcoholic for many years.
ex.ko: 그의 아버지는 오랫동안 알코올 중독자였다.

alike.en: very similar in appearance or nature

ex.en: The twins look exactly alike.
ex.ko: 그 쌍둥이는 정말 똑같이 생겼다.

alike.en: in the same way, applying equally to both

ex.en: The rule applies to students and teachers alike.
ex.ko: 그 규칙은 학생과 교사 모두에게 똑같이 적용된다.

allergic.en: having a bad physical reaction to something you eat, touch, or breathe

ex.en: I'm allergic to peanuts.
ex.ko: 나는 땅콩 알레르기가 있다.

allowance.en: an amount of money given regularly, especially to a child or as part of a job

ex.en: My parents give me a weekly allowance.
ex.ko: 부모님은 나에게 매주 용돈을 주신다.

aloud.en: using your voice so that people can hear you, rather than reading or thinking silently

ex.en: She read the letter aloud to her family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가족들에게 편지를 소리 내어 읽어 주었다.

alphabet.en: the set of letters used to write a language, in a fixed order

ex.en: Children learn the alphabet in kindergarten.
ex.ko: 아이들은 유치원에서 알파벳을 배운다.

alternative.en: one of two or more things that you can choose between

ex.en: Walking is a healthy alternative to driving.
ex.ko: 걷기는 운전에 대한 건강한 대안이다.

alternative.cue: 기존 것 대신 쓸 수 있는
alternative.en: available as another possibility or choice

ex.en: We need to find an alternative route.
ex.ko: 우리는 대체 경로를 찾아야 한다.

altogether.en: completely, or when everything is considered

ex.en: The trip cost 500 dollars altogether.
ex.ko: 그 여행은 전부 합쳐 500달러가 들었다.

amazed.en: feeling great surprise, often because something is impressive

ex.en: I was amazed by how quickly she learned the piano.
ex.ko: 그녀가 얼마나 빨리 피아노를 배웠는지 나는 놀랐다.

amazing.en: causing great surprise or wonder, especially because it is very impressive

ex.en: The view from the mountain was amazing.
ex.ko: 그 산에서 본 경치는 놀라웠다.

ambitious.en: having a strong desire to succeed or achieve something

ex.en: She is an ambitious young lawyer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 야심 찬 젊은 변호사이다.

ambulance.en: a vehicle used to take sick or injured people to the hospital

ex.en: They called an ambulance right after the accident.
ex.ko: 그들은 사고 직후 구급차를 불렀다.

amount.en: a quantity of something, especially money

ex.en: He spent a large amount of money on the car.
ex.ko: 그는 그 차에 많은 돈을 썼다.

amount.en: add up to a total, or turn out to be something in the end

ex.en: Her savings amounted to nearly a million won.
ex.ko: 그녀의 저축액은 거의 백만 원에 달했다.

amusing.en: causing you to laugh or smile; entertaining

ex.en: He told an amusing story about his trip.
ex.ko: 그는 여행에 관한 재미있는 이야기를 들려주었다.

analysis.en: a detailed study of something in order to understand it fully

ex.en: The report includes an analysis of last year's sales.
ex.ko: 그 보고서에는 작년 매출 분석이 포함되어 있다.

analyze.en: study something carefully in order to understand it

ex.en: Scientists analyzed the water samples for pollution.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 오염 여부를 알아보기 위해 물 샘플을 분석했다.

anger.en: a strong feeling of being upset or annoyed

ex.en: He couldn't hide his anger at the news.
ex.ko: 그는 그 소식에 대한 분노를 감출 수 없었다.

ex.en: His comments angered many fans.
ex.ko: 그의 발언은 많은 팬들을 화나게 했다.

angle.en: the space between two lines or surfaces that meet at a point, measured in degrees

ex.en: Measure the angle between the two walls.
ex.ko: 두 벽 사이의 각도를 측정해라.

angle.en: a particular way of looking at or thinking about a situation

ex.en: The reporter looked at the story from a new angle.
ex.ko: 그 기자는 새로운 관점에서 그 이야기를 다뤘다.

ex.en: They had an animated discussion about the movie.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 영화에 대해 활기찬 토론을 벌였다.

animated.en: made using a technique that makes drawings appear to move

ex.en: My kids love watching animated movies.
ex.ko: 우리 아이들은 애니메이션 영화 보는 것을 좋아한다.

animation.cue: 그림을 움직이는 것처럼 만든 영상 기법
animation.en: the technique of making films by using drawings, models, or computer graphics that appear to move

ex.en: She works in the animation industry.
ex.ko: 그녀는 애니메이션 업계에서 일한다.

announce.en: make something known publicly or officially

ex.en: The company announced a new product yesterday.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 어제 새 제품을 발표했다.

announcement.en: a public statement giving information about something

ex.en: There was an announcement about the flight delay.
ex.ko: 항공편 지연에 대한 공지가 있었다.

annoyance.en: the feeling of being slightly angry or bothered by something

ex.en: He couldn't hide his annoyance at the delay.
ex.ko: 그는 지연에 대한 짜증을 감출 수 없었다.

ex.en: She looked annoyed when the bus was late again.
ex.ko: 그녀는 버스가 또 늦자 짜증난 표정을 지었다.

ex.en: The company holds an annual meeting in March.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 3월에 연례 회의를 개최한다.

ex.en: The festival is held annually in the fall.
ex.ko: 그 축제는 매년 가을에 열린다.

ant.en: a small insect that lives in organized groups

ex.en: Ants were crawling all over the picnic food.
ex.ko: 개미들이 소풍 음식 위로 기어 다니고 있었다.

ex.en: He is anti almost every new policy the government suggests.
ex.ko: 그는 정부가 제안하는 거의 모든 새 정책에 반대한다.

antique.en: made a long time ago and valuable because of its age

ex.en: She collects antique furniture.
ex.ko: 그녀는 골동품 가구를 수집한다.

antique.en: an old object that is valuable because of its age, beauty, or rarity

ex.en: He bought a rare antique at the market.
ex.ko: 그는 시장에서 희귀한 골동품을 하나 샀다.

antonym.en: a word that means the opposite of another word

ex.en: 'Hot' is an antonym of 'cold'.
ex.ko: 'hot'은 'cold'의 반의어이다.

anxiety.en: an uncomfortable feeling of worry or nervousness about something that might happen

ex.en: She felt a lot of anxiety before the interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 면접 전에 큰 불안을 느꼈다.

anxiously.en: in a worried or nervous way

ex.en: He waited anxiously for the test results.
ex.ko: 그는 시험 결과를 불안하게 기다렸다.

anyhow.en: regardless of what has just been said; anyway

ex.en: It was raining, but we went hiking anyhow.
ex.ko: 비가 왔지만 우리는 어쨌든 등산을 갔다.

ape.en: a large primate with no tail, such as a gorilla or chimpanzee

ex.en: Apes are known for their intelligence.
ex.ko: 유인원은 지능이 높은 것으로 알려져 있다.

apology.cue: 잘못한 것에 대해 미안하다고 말하는 것
apology.en: a statement saying you are sorry for something you did wrong

ex.en: He owes her an apology for being so rude.
ex.ko: 그는 무례했던 것에 대해 그녀에게 사과해야 한다.

apparent.en: clear enough to be easily seen or understood

ex.en: It was apparent that she was upset.
ex.ko: 그녀가 화가 났다는 것이 분명했다.

apparent.en: seeming to be true, though it may not actually be so

ex.en: Despite his apparent calm, he was very nervous inside.
ex.ko: 겉보기에는 침착해 보였지만 그는 속으로 매우 긴장하고 있었다.

appeal.en: the quality of being attractive or interesting to people

ex.en: The movie has a strong appeal to young audiences.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 젊은 관객들에게 강한 매력을 지닌다.

appeal.en: make an urgent and serious request for help or support

ex.en: The charity appealed for donations after the disaster.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 재난 이후 기부를 호소했다.

appeal.en: formally ask a higher court to change a legal decision

ex.en: The company decided to appeal the court's decision.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 법원의 판결에 항소하기로 결정했다.

ex.en: Exercise can improve your appetite.
ex.ko: 운동은 식욕을 좋게 할 수 있다.

applaud.en: clap your hands to show approval or enjoyment

ex.en: The audience applauded loudly after the concert.
ex.ko: 관객들은 콘서트가 끝난 후 큰 박수를 쳤다.

applause.en: the sound of people clapping their hands to show approval

ex.en: The singer received loud applause at the end of the show.
ex.ko: 그 가수는 공연이 끝날 때 큰 박수를 받았다.

application.en: a formal written request for a job, place at a school, etc.

ex.en: She filled out a job application yesterday.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어제 입사 지원서를 작성했다.

application.en: a computer program designed for a particular purpose

ex.en: I downloaded a new application for tracking my expenses.
ex.ko: 나는 지출을 관리하는 새 앱을 다운로드했다.

application.en: the practical use of an idea, method, or discovery

ex.en: This theory has many applications in medicine.
ex.ko: 이 이론은 의학에서 다양하게 적용된다.

appoint.en: officially choose someone for a job or position

ex.en: She was appointed as the new manager.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 매니저로 임명되었다.

appreciation.en: a feeling of being grateful for something

ex.en: He showed his appreciation by writing a thank-you note.
ex.ko: 그는 감사 편지를 써서 고마움을 표현했다.

appreciation.en: an understanding of the value or quality of something

ex.en: She has a deep appreciation for classical music.
ex.ko: 그녀는 클래식 음악에 대한 깊은 이해를 가지고 있다.

approach.en: come nearer to someone or something in distance or time

ex.en: The storm is approaching the coast.
ex.ko: 폭풍이 해안에 다가오고 있다.

approach.en: a way of dealing with a problem or situation

ex.en: We need a new approach to solve this issue.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 문제를 해결할 새로운 접근법이 필요하다.

approval.en: official agreement to or acceptance of something

ex.en: The project needs approval from the board.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 이사회의 승인이 필요하다.

approve.en: officially agree to or accept something

ex.en: The city council approved the new building plan.
ex.ko: 시의회는 새 건축 계획을 승인했다.

approve.en: think that someone or something is good or acceptable

ex.en: Her parents don't approve of her new job.
ex.ko: 그녀의 부모님은 그녀의 새 직장을 좋게 여기지 않는다.

approximately.en: close to a particular number or amount, but not exact

ex.en: The trip takes approximately three hours.
ex.ko: 그 여행은 대략 세 시간이 걸린다.

architect.en: a person whose job is to design buildings

ex.en: The architect designed a beautiful new library.
ex.ko: 그 건축가는 아름다운 새 도서관을 설계했다.

arise.en: start to happen or come into existence

ex.en: A problem arose during the meeting.
ex.ko: 회의 중에 문제가 발생했다.

arithmetic.en: the part of mathematics that deals with adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing numbers

ex.en: The children practiced arithmetic every morning.
ex.ko: 아이들은 매일 아침 산수를 연습했다.

army.en: the part of a country's military forces that fights on land

ex.en: He joined the army right after high school.
ex.ko: 그는 고등학교를 졸업하자마자 군대에 입대했다.

arrange.en: make plans or preparations for something in advance

ex.en: She arranged a meeting with the client for Monday.
ex.ko: 그녀는 월요일에 고객과의 회의를 마련했다.

arrangement.en: a plan made in advance, or the way things are placed or organized

ex.en: We made arrangements for the wedding months ago.
ex.ko: 우리는 몇 달 전에 결혼식 준비를 해 두었다.

arrest.en: the act of taking someone into custody by police, especially for a crime

ex.en: The arrest was made late last night.
ex.ko: 체포는 어젯밤 늦게 이루어졌다.

arrest.en: take someone into custody, especially because they are suspected of a crime

ex.en: The police arrested the suspect at his home.
ex.ko: 경찰은 용의자를 그의 집에서 체포했다.

arrival.en: the act of arriving at a place

ex.en: We celebrated the arrival of the new year.
ex.ko: 우리는 새해의 도착을 축하했다.

arrow.en: a thin, straight stick shot from a bow

ex.en: The hunter shot an arrow at the deer.
ex.ko: 사냥꾼은 사슴을 향해 화살을 쐈다.

arrow.en: a symbol shaped like an arrow that shows direction

ex.en: Follow the arrows to find the exit.
ex.ko: 화살표를 따라가면 출구를 찾을 수 있다.

artist.en: a person who creates art, such as paintings, music, or writing

ex.en: She has always dreamed of becoming an artist.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 예술가가 되는 것을 꿈꿔 왔다.

artistic.en: relating to art, or showing skill and imagination in a creative way

ex.en: He has a very artistic style of painting.
ex.ko: 그는 매우 예술적인 그림 스타일을 가지고 있다.

ashamed.en: feeling embarrassed or guilty because of something you did

ex.en: He felt ashamed of lying to his friend.
ex.ko: 그는 친구에게 거짓말한 것이 부끄러웠다.

aside.en: to one side; out of the way, or kept separate for later use

ex.en: She stepped aside to let him pass.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그가 지나가도록 옆으로 비켜섰다.

aspect.en: a particular part or feature of a situation or subject

ex.en: Cost is an important aspect of the decision.
ex.ko: 비용은 그 결정에서 중요한 측면이다.

aspirin.en: a common medicine used to reduce pain and fever

ex.en: She took an aspirin for her headache.
ex.ko: 그녀는 두통 때문에 아스피린을 먹었다.

assign.en: give a particular task, job, or role to someone

ex.en: The teacher assigned each student a different topic.
ex.ko: 선생님은 각 학생에게 서로 다른 주제를 배정했다.

assignment.en: a piece of work or task given to someone

ex.en: I have to finish my history assignment by Friday.
ex.ko: 나는 금요일까지 역사 과제를 끝내야 한다.

assist.en: help someone by doing part of a task or providing support

ex.en: He assisted the nurse with the patients.
ex.ko: 그는 환자들을 돌보는 간호사를 도왔다.

assistance.en: help given to someone who needs it

ex.en: The organization provides assistance to homeless families.
ex.ko: 그 단체는 노숙자 가정에 도움을 제공한다.

associate.en: connect someone or something with someone or something else in your mind

ex.en: I always associate that song with summer.
ex.ko: 나는 그 노래를 항상 여름과 연관 짓는다.

associate.en: a person you work with or do business with

ex.en: He introduced her as his business associate.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀를 자신의 사업 동료로 소개했다.

assume.en: believe something to be true without proof

ex.en: I assumed she was coming, but she wasn't.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 올 거라고 추정했지만, 그녀는 오지 않았다.

assume.en: take on a duty, responsibility, or role

ex.en: She assumed the role of team leader last month.
ex.ko: 그녀는 지난달 팀장 역할을 맡았다.

astronomer.en: a scientist who studies stars, planets, and space

ex.en: The astronomer discovered a new planet.
ex.ko: 그 천문학자는 새로운 행성을 발견했다.

athletic.en: physically strong and good at sports

ex.en: He has an athletic build from years of training.
ex.ko: 그는 오랜 훈련으로 다져진 운동선수 같은 체격을 가지고 있다.

atlantic.en: relating to or bordering the Atlantic Ocean

ex.en: They took a cruise across the Atlantic Ocean.
ex.ko: 그들은 대서양을 가로지르는 크루즈 여행을 했다.

atmosphere.en: the layer of air surrounding the Earth

ex.en: Pollution is damaging the Earth's atmosphere.
ex.ko: 오염이 지구의 대기를 훼손하고 있다.

atmosphere.en: the general mood or feeling of a place or situation

ex.en: The restaurant has a cozy atmosphere.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 아늑한 분위기를 가지고 있다.

atomic.en: relating to atoms, the tiny particles that make up matter

ex.en: The atomic structure of the element was studied closely.
ex.ko: 그 원소의 원자 구조가 면밀히 연구되었다.

attach.en: fasten or connect one thing to another

ex.en: Please attach the file to your email.
ex.ko: 이메일에 파일을 첨부해 주세요.

attach.en: consider something to have a particular quality or importance

ex.en: She attaches great importance to punctuality.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시간 엄수에 큰 중요성을 부여한다.

attachment.en: a computer file sent along with an email

ex.en: I forgot to open the attachment in her email.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀의 이메일에 있는 첨부 파일을 여는 것을 잊었다.

attachment.en: a strong feeling of affection or connection to someone or something

ex.en: He has a deep attachment to his childhood home.
ex.ko: 그는 어린 시절 집에 깊은 애착을 가지고 있다.

attain.en: succeed in reaching a goal or level, usually through effort

ex.en: She attained a high score on the exam.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시험에서 높은 점수를 달성했다.

attend.en: be present at an event, meeting, or place

ex.en: She attended the conference last week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 지난주 그 학회에 참석했다.

attend.en: deal with or take care of something

ex.en: He needs to attend to some urgent business first.
ex.ko: 그는 먼저 급한 업무를 처리해야 한다.

attract.en: cause someone or something to be interested or come nearer

ex.en: The new store attracted many customers.
ex.ko: 그 새 가게는 많은 손님을 끌어들였다.

attraction.en: the quality of being interesting or attractive to someone

ex.en: There was an instant attraction between them.
ex.ko: 그들 사이에는 즉각적인 끌림이 있었다.

attraction.en: a place or event that people visit because it is interesting or enjoyable

ex.en: The museum is a popular tourist attraction.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 인기 있는 관광 명소이다.

authority.en: the power or right to give orders and make decisions

ex.en: The manager has the authority to approve the budget.
ex.ko: 그 매니저는 예산을 승인할 권한이 있다.

authority.en: the government or people who have official power to control and check things

ex.en: The authorities closed the road after the accident.
ex.ko: 당국은 사고 이후 도로를 폐쇄했다.

authority.cue: 특정 분야를 매우 잘 아는 사람
authority.en: a person who is an expert in a particular subject

ex.en: She is a leading authority on climate change.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기후 변화 분야의 대표적인 권위자이다.
